content.overview:
Mental health is a state of well-being in which every individual realises their own potential, can cope with normal stresses of life, can work productively and fruitfully, and is able to make a contribution to their community. It is an integral part of health — there is no health without mental health. Mental health conditions are common, treatable, and nothing to be ashamed of.

- 1 in 8 people globally live with a mental disorder.
- Depression and anxiety are among the most common conditions.
- In India, the National Mental Health Survey (2016) found that about 10.6% of adults have a mental health condition.
- Suicide is the second leading cause of death among 15–29 year olds globally.
- 75% of people with mental health conditions in low and middle income countries receive no treatment.
- Mental health conditions are treatable — early support makes a significant difference.

--- IMPORTANT SAFETY NOTE ---
If you or someone you know is in a mental health crisis or expressing suicidal thoughts, please contact emergency services (112) or a crisis helpline immediately. This chatbot is for awareness only and is not a substitute for professional mental health care.
types: Persistent sadness, loss of interest or pleasure in activities
Feelings of worthlessness or excessive guilt
In severe cases, thoughts of self-harm or suicide
Depression is NOT just sadness — it is a medical condition and is treatable.
Avoiding certain situations due to fear
Includes panic disorder, social anxiety, phobias, and generalised anxiety disorder
Feeling overwhelmed or unable to cope
Physical symptoms: headache, muscle tension, stomach issues
Manageable with lifestyle changes and support
Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
symptoms: Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
warning_signs: Prolonged feelings of sadness, emptiness, or hopelessness
Withdrawing from friends, family, or activities they used to enjoy
Significant changes in sleep, appetite, or energy
Difficulty performing daily tasks (school, work, self-care)
Increased use of alcohol or substances
Talking about wanting to die or harming oneself
Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
risk_factors: Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
prevention: Regular physical activity — even 30 minutes of walking improves mood
Healthy diet — reduces anxiety and improves mood
Setting realistic goals and managing workload
Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
environmental_control: Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
vaccination: Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
diagnosis: Not separately specified or not applicable in the available source text for this topic.
treatment_awareness_only: Talk to someone you trust — a friend, family member, teacher, or mentor.
Seek help from a mental health professional: psychiatrist, psychologist, or counsellor.
Government hospitals have free psychiatric outpatient services.
iCall helpline (TISS): 9152987821 — free counselling for students and young adults.
NIMHANS (Bengaluru) offers specialised mental health services: nimhans.ac.in.
Avoid saying "just cheer up" or "others have it worse"
Encourage them to seek professional help
Stay in touch and check in regularly
In crisis situations, do not leave them alone — contact emergency services (112)
Treatment details are for awareness only. Consult a qualified healthcare professional.
when_to_see_a_doctor: Seek medical care for severe, persistent, worsening, or red-flag symptoms. Use emergency number 112 for life-threatening situations.
india_specific_context: The National Mental Health Programme (NMHP) aims to ensure availability and accessibility of minimum mental health care for all in the foreseeable future. District Mental Health Programme (DMHP) provides community mental health services at the district level including awareness, early detection, and treatment.
Confirm current India-specific guidance through MoHFW, NHM, NHP, ICMR/NCDC, NIMHANS, or relevant state health services where applicable.
